title: Faulted bulk drive in a degraded RAIDZ2 vdev
description: mpathv (Seagate ST4000NM0023) FAULTED in raidz2-2; that vdev now has one of two parity drives left. IMPACT CORRECTED 2026-07-23: bulk is near-empty (E-29), so this is loss of redundancy, not imminent data loss. Drive is fully offline (no /dev/mapper entry, both SAS paths dead); ZFS holds it by cached label.
evidence: E-04 live zpool status; E-29 zfs list; NF-INC-2026-07-22 slot15
existing_controls: RAIDZ2 tolerates 1 loss; 2 free bays; scrub clean; ZfsDeviceFaulted alert now live (E-27)
treatment: Replace mpathv (zpool replace) + scrub - prudent maintenance to restore double parity
validation: Live zpool status + zfs list + audit corroboration

title: bulk pool has no second copy
affected_assets: bulk/backups (Ares restic + jarvis-netframe); future research data
description: bulk is neither a PBS source nor target. IMPACT CORRECTED 2026-07-23 (E-29): fernanda/media/misc/archive are 205-222 KB placeholders; the only real data is bulk/backups 57.3 GB, itself secondary copies whose sources still exist. DUNE research data has NOT yet migrated to bulk. The exposure becomes real WHEN it does, so a second copy must exist BEFORE that migration. Note the Ares restic backup currently targets this degraded pool.
evidence: E-29 zfs list -r bulk; storage.cfg (bulk absent)
existing_controls: raidz2 redundancy; sources of bulk/backups still exist
treatment: Establish the second copy before research data migrates onto bulk; then add bulk/fernanda to PBS or rclone->B2

title: ZFS device-level fault invisible to alerting
description: ROOT-CAUSED 2026-07-23: not a delivery failure. The collector emitted only node_zfs_pool_health from 'zpool list -o health', which returns ONLINE for bulk because raidz2 retains redundancy. max_over_time(node_zfs_pool_health{pool=bulk}[7d])=0, so ZfsPoolDegraded (fires on >0) could never trigger. No per-device metric existed at all - a faulted disk in a still-redundant pool was a monitoring blind spot.
evidence: E-27 Prometheus range query; E-26 collector validation; NF-INC-2026-07-22
treatment: REMEDIATED: added node_zfs_pool_nonhealthy_devices + critical ZfsDeviceFaulted rule (>0 for 5m); deployed to randy/quarkylab/jarvis; verified live bulk=1, others 0
validation: Prometheus query post-deploy (E-27); reviewer did not independently confirm Grafana rule load

title: STP root elected by MAC accident rather than design
affected_assets: EX3400 core switch, entire L2 domain
description: Found 2026-07-23: no bridge-priority was configured anywhere; the EX3400 sat at default 32768 and the UniFi USW-24-250W (b4:fb:e4:1b:ca:3c) had won root via lower MAC, with the core reaching it at cost 20000 over a 1G link. Docs asserted the EX3400 was root at priority 4096, which was false. Root placement could move unpredictably on reboot or topology change, and traffic paths were suboptimal.
evidence: E-22 show spanning-tree bridge; E-25 config (no bridge-priority)
existing_controls: RSTP running; bpdu-block-on-edge set; single member VC
treatment: REMEDIATED: set protocols rstp bridge-priority 4096 committed; Root ID==Bridge ID==4096.c0:42:d0:f9:ec:71; ge-0/0/46 role ROOT->DESG. First attempt auto-reverted (commit confirmed not confirmed) - safety net worked as designed.
